member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel
member.summary: A certificate is a document stored in either AvaTax Exemptions or CertCapture. The certificate document can contain information about a customer's eligibility for exemption from sales or use taxes based on criteria you specify when you store the certificate. To view or manage your certificates directly, please log onto the administrative website for the product you purchased.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.companyId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the AvaTax company that recorded this certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.signedDate
member.summary: The date when this certificate was signed.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.expirationDate
member.summary: Expiration date when this certificate will no longer be valid.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.filename
member.summary:
File name for the image of this certificate.
When creating a certificate, if you do not upload a PDF or JPG image, you must specify the filename of the certificate as it is tracked in your repository.
To create a certificate, you must provide one of the following fields: either a `filename`, a `pdf` file, or an array of JPG `pages`. The API will return an error if you omit these fields or if you attempt to put values in more than one of them.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.documentExists
member.summary: This value is true if there exists scanned PDF copy of this certificate or the PDF version of the form that the customer filled via the CertCapture wizard on S3 bucket.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.valid
member.summary: True if this certificate is marked as valid. A valid certificate can be considered for exemption purposes. When a certificate is marked invalid, it will no longer be considered when calculating exemption for a customer.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.verified
member.summary: This value is true if the certificate has gone through the certificate validation process. For more information on the certificate validation process, please see the Avalara Help Center.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.exemptPercentage
member.summary:
If this certificate provides exemption from transactional taxes, what percentage of the transaction is considered exempt?
For a fully exempt certificate, this percentage should be 100.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.isSingleCertificate
member.summary: This value is true if this certificate is a single (or standalone) certificate. This value is set during the audit stage of the certificate validation process.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.exemptionNumber
member.summary: Indicates the tax number passed in for the certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.status
member.summary: The status of the certificate. Possible values for status COMPLETE,PENDING,PENDING-FUTURE,PENDING-MULTI,PENDING-SINGLE,REVOKED

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.ecmStatus
member.summary: The status of the certificate as displayed on https://exemptions.avalara.com. The values in `CertificateEcmStatus` include all the possible status values.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.createdDate
member.summary: The date/time when this record was created.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.modifiedDate
member.summary: The date/time when this record was last modified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.taxNumberType
member.summary: The tax number type for the certificate. For example, `FEIN`, `Social Security Number`, or `Employer Identification Number`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.businessNumberType
member.summary: Description of business for the certificate. For example, `Retail trade`, `Professional services`, or `Construction`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.pageCount
member.summary: The number of pages contained within this certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.customers
member.summary: A list of customers to which this certificate applies. You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=customers` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.poNumbers
member.summary:
A list of purchase order numbers that are valid for use with this certificate.
If this certificate is applicable for all purchase order numbers, this field will be empty.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=po_numbers` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.attributes
member.summary:
A list of certificate attributes that apply to this certificate.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=attributes` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.histories
member.summary:
A list of update histories for this certificate.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=histories` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.jobs
member.summary:
A list of jobs for this certificate.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=jobs` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.logs
member.summary:
A list of logs for this certificate.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=logs` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.invalidReasons
member.summary:
For a certificate with an invalid status, this lists the reasons why the certificate is invalid.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=invalid_reasons` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.customFields
member.summary:
A list of custom defined fields for this certificate.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=custom_fields` when calling a certificate fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.ecmsId
member.summary: The unique ID number of current AvaTax Exemption Certificate that refers this certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.ecmsStatus
member.summary: The status of current AvaTax Exemption Certificate that refers to this certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.pdf
member.summary:
This field is available for input only. To retrieve the image after creation, use the `DownloadCertificateImage` API.
When creating a certificate, you may optionally provide a PDF image in Base64 URLEncoded format. PDFs are automatically parsed into individual page JPG images and can be retrieved back later as either the original PDF or the individual pages.
To create a certificate, you must provide one of the following fields: either a `filename`, a `pdf` file, or an array of JPG `pages`. The API will return an error if you omit these fields or if you attempt to put values in more than one of them.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.pages
member.summary:
This field is available for input only. To retrieve the image after creation, use the `DownloadCertificateImage` API.
When creating a certificate, you may optionally provide a list of JPG images, one per page, in Base64 URLEncoded format. These JPG images are automatically combined into a single downloadable PDF and can be retrieved back later as either the original JPG images or the combined PDF.
To create a certificate, you must provide one of the following fields: either a `filename`, a `pdf` file, or an array of JPG `pages`. The API will return an error if you omit these fields or if you attempt to put values in more than one of them.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.summary: Represents a tax code used by the CertCapture process

member.summary: ID number of the tax code

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CertificateTaxCodeModel.name
member.summary: Name of the tax code

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel
member.summary:
A company-distance-threshold model indicates the distance between a company and the taxing borders of various countries. Distance thresholds are necessary to correctly calculate some value-added taxes.
Distance thresholds only apply to sales of goods in certain countries. A distance threshold is applied for each ship-from/ship-to combination of countries. The threshold amount is defined by the ship-to country.
Generally, if you have exceeded a distance threshold for taxes between a pair of countries, your tax calculation will be determined to be the rate in the destination country. If you have not exceeded the threshold, your tax calculation will be determined to be the rate in the origin country.
The amount of a threshold is not tracked or managed in AvaTax, but the decision of your tax compliance department as to whether you have exceeded this threshold is maintained in this object.
By default, you are considered to have exceeded tax thresholds. If you wish to change this default, you can create a company-distance-threshold object to select the correct behavior for this origin/destination tax calculation process.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.id
member.summary: A unique ID number representing this distance threshold object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.companyId
member.summary: The ID number of the company that defined this distance threshold.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.originCountry
member.summary:
The origin country for this threshold.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.destinationCountry
member.summary:
The destination country for this threshold.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.effDate
member.summary: For distance threshold values that change over time, this is the earliest date for which this distance threshold is valid. If null, this distance threshold is valid for all dates earlier than the `endDate` field.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.endDate
member.summary: For distance threshold values that change over time, this is the latest date for which this distance threshold is valid. If null, this distance threshold is valid for all dates later than the `effDate` field.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.thresholdExceeded
member.summary:
True if your tax professional has determined that the value-added tax distance threshold is exceeded for this pair of countries.
If you set this value to `false`, your value added taxes will be calculated using the origin country. Otherwise, value added taxes will be calculated on the destination country.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.type
member.summary:
Indicates the distance threshold type.
This value can be either `Sale` or `Purchase`.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyDistanceThresholdModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel
member.summary: A company or business entity.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.accountId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the account this company belongs to.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.parentCompanyId
member.summary: If this company is fully owned by another company, this is the unique identity of the parent company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.sstPid
member.summary: If this company files Streamlined Sales Tax, this is the PID of this company as defined by the Streamlined Sales Tax governing board.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.companyCode
member.summary: A unique code that references this company within your account.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.name
member.summary: The name of this company, as shown to customers.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.isDefault
member.summary: This flag is true if this company is the default company for this account. Only one company may be set as the default.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.defaultLocationId
member.summary: If set, this is the unique ID number of the default location for this company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.isActive
member.summary: This flag indicates whether tax activity can occur for this company. Set this flag to true to permit the company to process transactions.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.taxpayerIdNumber
member.summary: For United States companies, this field contains your Taxpayer Identification Number. This is a nine digit number that is usually called an EIN for an Employer Identification Number if this company is a corporation, or SSN for a Social Security Number if this company is a person. This value is required if the address provided is inside the US and if you subscribed to the Avalara Managed Returns or SST Certified Service Provider service. Otherwise it is optional.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.isFein
member.summary: Set this field to true if the taxPayerIdNumber is a FEIN.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.hasProfile
member.summary: Set this flag to true to give this company its own unique tax profile. If this flag is true, this company will have its own Nexus, TaxRule, TaxCode, and Item definitions. If this flag is false, this company will inherit all profile values from its parent.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.isReportingEntity
member.summary: Set this flag to true if this company must file its own tax returns. For users who have Returns enabled, this flag turns on monthly Worksheet generation for the company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.sstEffectiveDate
member.summary: If this company participates in Streamlined Sales Tax, this is the date when the company joined the SST program.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.defaultCountry
member.summary: The two character ISO-3166 country code of the default country for this company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.baseCurrencyCode
member.summary: This is the three character ISO-4217 currency code of the default currency used by this company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.roundingLevelId
member.summary: Indicates whether this company prefers to round amounts at the document level or line level.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.warningsEnabled
member.summary: Set this value to true to receive warnings in API calls via SOAP.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.isTest
member.summary: Set this flag to true to indicate that this company is a test company. If you have Returns enabled, Test companies will not file tax returns and can be used for validation purposes.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.taxDependencyLevelId
member.summary: Used to apply tax detail dependency at a jurisdiction level.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.inProgress
member.summary: Set this value to true to indicate that you are still working to finish configuring this company. While this value is true, no tax reporting will occur and the company will not be usable for transactions.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CompanyModel.contacts
member.summary:
Optional: A list of contacts defined for this company. To fetch this list, add the query string `?$include=Contacts` to your URL.
When calling `CreateCompany`, you may provide a list of objects in this element and they will be created alongside the company. The `UpdateCompany` API does not permit updating nested objects.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ComplianceJurisdictionModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ComplianceJurisdictionModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country of this jurisdiction.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel
member.summary: Represents an invitation for a customer to use CertExpress to self-report their own certificates. This invitation is delivered by your choice of method, or you can present a hyperlink to the user directly in your connector. Your customer will be redirected to https://app.certexpress.com/ where they can follow a step-by-step guide to enter information about their exemption certificates. The certificates entered will be recorded and automatically linked to their customer record.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel.recipient
member.summary: If the value of `deliveryMethod` is set to `Email`, please specify the email address of the recipient for the request.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel.coverLetterTitle
member.summary: If this invitation is sent via email or download, please specify the cover letter to use when building this invitation. For a list of cover letters, please call `ListCoverLetters`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel.exposureZones
member.summary:
You may optionally specify a list of exposure zones to request in this CertExpress invitation. If you list more than one exposure zone, the customer will be prompted to provide an exemption certificate for each one. If you do not provide a list of exposure zones, the customer will be prompted to select an exposure zone.
For a list of available exposure zones, please call `ListCertificateExposureZones`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel.exemptReasons
member.summary:
You may optionally specify a list of exemption reasons to pre-populate in this CertExpress invitation. If you list exemption reasons, the customer will have part of their form already filled in when they visit the CertExpress website.
For a list of available exemption reasons, please call `ListCertificateExemptReasons`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel.deliveryMethod
member.summary:
Specify the type of invitation. CertExpress invitations can be delivered via email, web link, or facsimile.
* If you specify `Email`, the invitation will be delivered via email. Please ask the customer to ensure that * If you specify `Fax`, the invitation will be sent via fax to the customer's fax number on file. * If you specify `Download`, the invitation will be prepared as a web link that you can display to the customer.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateCertExpressInvitationModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel
member.summary: Create a multi company transaction

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.code
member.summary: Transaction Code - the internal reference code used by the client application. This is used for operations such as Get, Adjust, Settle, and Void. If you leave the transaction code blank, a GUID will be assigned to each transaction. In multi company scenario, each transaction with be this code with an extension at the end, ".1", ".2", ".3" etc

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.lines
member.summary: Multi company transaction line item list

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.type
member.summary:
Specifies the type of document to create. A document type ending with `Invoice` is a permanent transaction that will be recorded in AvaTax. A document type ending with `Order` is a temporary estimate that will not be preserved.
If you omit this value, the API will assume you want to create a `SalesOrder`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.companyCode
member.summary: Company Code - Specify the code of the company creating this transaction here. If you leave this value null, your account's default company will be used instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.date
member.summary:
Transaction Date - The date on the invoice, purchase order, etc.
By default, this date will be used to calculate the tax rates for the transaction. If you wish to use a different date to calculate tax rates, please specify a `taxOverride` of type `taxDate`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.salespersonCode
member.summary: Salesperson Code - The client application salesperson reference code.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.customerCode
member.summary: Customer Code - The client application customer reference code.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: Customer Usage Type - The client application customer or usage type. For a list of available usage types, see `/api/v2/definitions/entityusecodes`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.discount
member.summary: Discount - The discount amount to apply to the document. This value will be applied only to lines that have the `discounted` flag set to true. If no lines have `discounted` set to true, this discount cannot be applied.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.purchaseOrderNo
member.summary:
Purchase Order Number for this document.
This is required for single use exemption certificates to match the order and invoice with the certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.exemptionNo
member.summary:
Exemption Number for this document.
If you specify an exemption number for this document, this document will be considered exempt, and you may be asked to provide proof of this exemption certificate in the event that you are asked by an auditor to verify your exemptions.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.addresses
member.summary:
Default addresses for all lines in this document.
These addresses are the default values that will be used for any lines that do not have their own address information. If you specify addresses for a line, then no default addresses will be loaded for that line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.parameters
member.summary:
Special parameters for this transaction.
To get a full list of available parameters, please use the `/api/v2/definitions/parameters` endpoint.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.referenceCode
member.summary:
Customer-provided Reference Code with information about this transaction.
This field could be used to reference the original document for a return invoice, or for any other reference purpose.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.reportingLocationCode
member.summary:
Sets the sale location code (Outlet ID) for reporting this document to the tax authority.
This value is used by Avalara Managed Returns to group documents together by reporting locations for tax authorities that require location-based reporting.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.commit
member.summary: Causes the document to be committed if true. This option is only applicable for invoice document types, not orders.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.batchCode
member.summary: BatchCode for batch operations.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.taxOverride
member.summary: Specifies a tax override for the entire document

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.currencyCode
member.summary: The three-character ISO 4217 currency code for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.serviceMode
member.summary: Specifies whether the tax calculation is handled Local, Remote, or Automatic (default). This only applies when using an AvaLocal server.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.exchangeRate
member.summary:
Currency exchange rate from this transaction to the company base currency.
This only needs to be set if the transaction currency is different than the company base currency. It defaults to 1.0.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.exchangeRateEffectiveDate
member.summary: Effective date of the exchange rate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.posLaneCode
member.summary: Sets the Point of Sale Lane Code sent by the User for this document.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.businessIdentificationNo
member.summary:
VAT business identification number for the customer for this transaction. This number will be used for all lines in the transaction, except for those lines where you have defined a different business identification number.
If you specify a VAT business identification number for the customer in this transaction and you have also set up a business identification number for your company during company setup, this transaction will be treated as a business-to-business transaction for VAT purposes and it will be calculated according to VAT tax rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiCompanyTransactionModel.isSellerImporterOfRecord

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel
member.summary:
A MultiDocument transaction represents a sale or purchase that occurred between more than two companies.
A traditional transaction requires exactly two parties: a seller and a buyer. MultiDocument transactions can involve a marketplace of vendors, each of which contributes some portion of the final transaction. Within a MultiDocument transaction, each individual buyer and seller pair are matched up and converted to a separate document. This separation of documents allows each seller to file their taxes separately.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.code
member.summary:
The transaction code of the MultiDocument transaction.
All individual transactions within this MultiDocument object will have this code as a prefix.
If you leave the `code` field blank, a GUID will be assigned.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.lines
member.summary:
Lines that will appear on the invoice.
For a MultiDocument transaction, each line may represent a different company or reporting location code. AvaTax will separate this MultiDocument transaction object into many different transactions, one for each pair of legal entities, so that each legal entity can file their transactional taxes correctly.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.allowAdjust
member.summary:
Set this value to true to allow this API call to adjust the MultiDocument model if one already exists.
If you omit this field, or if the value is `null`, you will receive an error if you try to create two MultiDocument objects with the same `code`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.type
member.summary:
Specifies the type of document to create. A document type ending with `Invoice` is a permanent transaction that will be recorded in AvaTax. A document type ending with `Order` is a temporary estimate that will not be preserved.
If you omit this value, the API will assume you want to create a `SalesOrder`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.companyCode
member.summary: Company Code - Specify the code of the company creating this transaction here. If you leave this value null, your account's default company will be used instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.date
member.summary:
Transaction Date - The date on the invoice, purchase order, etc. AvaTax accepts date values in [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601) format and stores the date as `yyyy-MM-dd`.
By default, this date will be used to calculate the tax rates for the transaction. If you want to use a different date to calculate tax rates, please specify a `taxOverride` of type `taxDate`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.salespersonCode
member.summary: Salesperson Code - The client application salesperson reference code.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.customerCode
member.summary: Customer Code - The client application customer reference code. Note: This field is case sensitive. To have exemption certificates apply, this value should be the same as the one passed to create a customer.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 10/16/2017, Version: 17.11, Message: Please use entityUseCode instead. Customer Usage Type - The client application customer or usage type.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.entityUseCode
member.summary: Entity Use Code - The client application customer or usage type. For a list of available usage types, use [ListEntityUseCodes](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListEntityUseCodes/) API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.discount
member.summary: Discount - The discount amount to apply to the document. This value will be applied only to lines that have the `discounted` flag set to true. If no lines have `discounted` set to true, this discount cannot be applied.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.purchaseOrderNo
member.summary:
Purchase Order Number for this document.
This is required for single use exemption certificates to match the order and invoice with the certificate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.exemptionNo
member.summary:
Exemption Number for this document.
If you specify an exemption number for this document, this document will be considered exempt, and you may be asked to provide proof of this exemption certificate in the event that you are asked by an auditor to verify your exemptions. Note: This is same as 'exemptNo' in TransactionModel.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.parameters
member.summary:
Special parameters for this transaction.
To get a full list of available parameters, please use the [ListParameters](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListParameters/) endpoint.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.userDefinedFields
member.summary: Custom user fields/flex fields for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.referenceCode
member.summary:
Customer-provided Reference Code with information about this transaction.
This field could be used to reference the original document for a return invoice, or for any other reference purpose.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.reportingLocationCode
member.summary:
Sets the sale location code (Outlet ID) for reporting this document to the tax authority.
This value is used by Avalara Managed Returns to group documents together by reporting locations for tax authorities that require location-based reporting.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.commit
member.summary: Causes the document to be committed if true. This option is only applicable for invoice document types, not orders.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.batchCode
member.summary: BatchCode for batch operations.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.currencyCode
member.summary: The three-character ISO 4217 currency code representing the ‘invoice currency’ (the currency the transaction was invoiced in). If this is different than the currency the tax liability needs to be reported in, you’ll also need to provide the `exchangeRateCurrencyCode` and the `exchangeRate` for conversion to the reporting country.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.serviceMode
member.summary: Specifies whether the tax calculation is handled Local, Remote, or Automatic (default). This only applies when using an AvaLocal server.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.exchangeRate
member.summary: The currency exchange rate from the invoice currency (`currencyCode`) to the reporting currency (`exchangeRateCurrencyCode`). This only needs to be set if the invoice currency and the reporting currency are different. It defaults to 1.0.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.exchangeRateEffectiveDate
member.summary: Effective date of the exchange rate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.exchangeRateCurrencyCode
member.summary: The three-character ISO 4217 currency code representing the ‘reporting currency’ (the currency the transaction’s tax liability needs to be reported in). You can leave this blank if the invoice currency provided in the `currencyCode` field is also the reporting currency.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.posLaneCode
member.summary: Sets the Point of Sale Lane Code sent by the User for this document.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.businessIdentificationNo
member.summary:
VAT business identification number for the customer for this transaction. This number will be used for all lines in the transaction, except for those lines where you have defined a different business identification number.
If you specify a VAT business identification number for the customer in this transaction and you have also set up a business identification number for your company during company setup, this transaction will be treated as a business-to-business transaction for VAT purposes and it will be calculated according to VAT tax rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.isSellerImporterOfRecord
member.summary:
Specifies if the transaction should have value-added and cross-border taxes calculated with the seller as the importer of record.
Some taxes only apply if the seller is the importer of record for a product. In cases where companies are working together to ship products, there may be mutual agreement as to which company is the entity designated as importer of record. The importer of record will then be the company designated to pay taxes marked as being obligated to the importer of record.
Set this value to `true` to consider your company as the importer of record and collect these taxes.
This value may also be set at the Nexus level. See `NexusModel` for more information.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.description
member.summary: User-supplied description for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.email
member.summary: User-supplied email address relevant for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.debugLevel
member.summary: If the user wishes to request additional debug information from this transaction, specify a level higher than `normal`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.customerSupplierName
member.summary: The name of the supplier / exporter / seller. For sales doctype enter the name of your own company for which you are reporting. For purchases doctype enter the name of the supplier you have purchased from.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.dataSourceId
member.summary: The Id of the datasource from which this transaction originated. This value will be overridden by the system to take the datasource Id from the call header.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CreateMultiDocumentModel.deliveryTerms
member.summary:
Delivery Terms (or Incoterms) refer to agreed-upon conditions between a buyer and a seller for the delivery of goods. They are three-letter trade terms that describe the practical arrangements for the delivery of goods from sellers to buyers and set out the obligations, costs, and risks between the two parties. The DeliveryTerms field determines who acts as the Importer of Record and who arranges the Transport of the goods when this information is not separately sent to AvaTax in the request. When used in conjunction with isSellerImporterOfRecord, this parameter can also influence whether AvaTax includes Import Duty and Tax in the transaction totals. If the fields for transport or isSellerImporterOfRecord are passed in the request and conflict with the DeliveryTerms, the values provided in the first will take priority over the DeliveryTerms parameter. If neither transport nor isSellerImporterOfRecord is passed in the request and DeliveryTerms is passed, AvaTax will determine who acts as Importer of Record and who arranges the Transport of the goods based on the specified DeliveryTerms. If none of these fields is passed, AvaTax will keep the current behavior and default transport to "Seller" for goods and isSellerImporterOfRecord to "False" (i.e., the AvaTax user does not act as Importer of record)." Finally, this field is also used for reports.
The Delivery Terms that the user can pass are the following: 1. Ex Works (EXW) 2. Free Carrier (FCA) 3. Carrier and Insurance Paid to (CIP) 4. Carriage Paid To (CPT) 5. Delivered at Place (DAP) 6. Delivered at Place Unloaded (DPU) 7. Delivered Duty Paid (DDP) 8. Free Alongside Ship (FAS) 9. Free on Board (FOB) 10. Cost and Freight (CFR) 11. Cost, Insurance and Freight (CIF)
DAP and DDP are two delivery terms that indicate that Import Duty and Tax should be included in the transaction total.

member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel
member.summary: Represents a customer to whom you sell products and/or services.

member.summary: The unique ID number of this customer.

member.summary: The unique ID number of the AvaTax company that recorded this customer.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.customerCode
member.summary:
The unique code identifying this customer. Must be unique within your company.
This code should be used in the `customerCode` field of any call that creates or adjusts a transaction in order to ensure that all exemptions that apply to this customer are correctly considered.
Note: This field is case sensitive.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.alternateId
member.summary: A customer-configurable alternate ID number for this customer. You may set this value to match any other system that would like to reference this customer record.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.name
member.summary: A friendly name identifying this customer.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.attnName
member.summary: Indicates the "Attn:" component of the address for this customer, if this customer requires mailings to be shipped to the attention of a specific person or department name.

member.summary: Postal Code / Zip Code component of the address of this customer.

member.summary: Date when this customer last executed a transaction.

member.summary: The date when this record was created.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.modifiedDate
member.summary: The date/time when this record was last modified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.region
member.summary: ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country. Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes. This is a required field if the country is US or CA. For other countries, this is an optional field. For a full list of all supported codes, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.isBill
member.summary: True if this customer record is specifically used for bill-to purposes.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.isShip
member.summary: True if this customer record is specifically used for ship-to purposes.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.taxpayerIdNumber
member.summary: For customers in the United States, this field is the federal taxpayer ID number. For businesses, this is a Federal Employer Identification Number. For individuals, this will be a Social Security Number.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.certificates
member.summary: A list of exemption certficates that apply to this customer. You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=certificates` when calling a customer fetch API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.customFields
member.summary:
A list of custom fields defined on this customer.
For more information about custom fields, see the [Avalara Help Center article about custom fields](https://help.avalara.com/0021_Avalara_CertCapture/All_About_CertCapture/Edit_or_Remove_Details_about_Customers).

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.exposureZones
member.summary:
A list of exposure zones where you do business with this customer.
To keep track of certificates that are needed for each customer, set this value to a list of all exposure zones where you sell products to this customer. You can find a list of exposure zones by calling `ListExposureZones`.
This field is often called "Ship-To States" or "Ship-To Zones", since it generally refers to locations where you ship products when this customer makes a purchase.
This field is useful for audit purposes since it helps you ensure you have the necessary certificates for each customer.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.billTos
member.summary:
A list of bill-to customer records that are connected to this ship-to customer.
Customer records represent businesses or individuals who can provide exemption certificates. Some customers may have certificates that are linked to their shipping address or their billing address. To group these customer records together, you may link multiple bill-to and ship-to addresses together to represent a single entity that has multiple different addresses of different kinds.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.shipTos
member.summary:
A list of ship-to customer records that are connected to this bill-to customer.
Customer records represent businesses or individuals who can provide exemption certificates. Some customers may have certificates that are linked to their shipping address or their billing address. To group these customer records together, you may link multiple bill-to and ship-to addresses together to represent a single entity that has multiple different addresses of different kinds.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomerModel.attributes
member.summary:
A list of attributes that apply to this customer.
You can fetch this data by specifying `$include=attributes` when calling a customer fetch API.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.CustomFieldModel
member.summary:
A custom field provides extra information about a customer or certificate.
Custom fields are provided to permit you to store additional information about an exemption certificate or customer. They are available to support additional use cases beyond that supported directly by Avalara's exemption certificate software.
For more information about custom fields, see the [Avalara Help Center article about custom fields](https://help.avalara.com/0021_Avalara_CertCapture/All_About_CertCapture/Edit_or_Remove_Details_about_Customers).

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel
member.summary: Use this object to provide an address and date range where your company does business. This address will be used to determine what jurisdictions you should declare nexus and calculate tax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel.effectiveDate
member.summary: The earliest date on which your company does business at this address. If you omit a value in this field, nexus will be declared at the earliest possible date for this jurisdiction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel.endDate
member.summary: The date on which your company stopped doing business at this address, or empty if your company has no plans to stop doing business at this address.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel.taxTypeGroup
member.summary:
The type group of nexus that this company is declaring Use [ListTaxTypeGroups](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListTaxTypeGroups/) API for a list of nexus tax type groups.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`. NOTE: This optional field will trigger nexus subtype lookup when populated. When using make sure TaxTypeGroup matches corresponding NexusTaxTypeGroup

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel.nexusTaxTypeGroup
member.summary:
The type of nexus that this company is declaring.Replaces NexusTypeId. Use [ListNexusTaxTypeGroups](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListNexusTaxTypeGroups/) API for a list of nexus tax type groups.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.summary: City component of the address

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.DeclareNexusByAddressModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.summary: An input model for executing a report detailed to the document line level

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.format
member.summary: The file format.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.startDate
member.summary: The start date filter for report execution. If no date provided, same date of last month will be used as the startDate. Accepts date in short format yyyy-mm-dd as well as date time stamp

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.endDate
member.summary: The end date filter for report execution. If no date provided, today's date will be used as the endDate. Accepts date in short format yyyy-mm-dd as well as date time stamp

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.country
member.summary: The transactions in the country you wish to run a report. Use "ALL" for all countries Use "ALL Non-US" for all international countries Or use a single 2-char ISO country code

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.state
member.summary: The state associated with the transactions you wish to run a report. Use "ALL" for all states or 2-char state code.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.dateFilter
member.summary: The type of date to filter your transactions

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.docType
member.summary: The transaction type you want to run a report on

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.currencyCode
member.summary: The currency your report is displayed in. Accepts "USD" or leave this blank to get all the documents with all the currencies

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.numberOfPartitions
member.summary: Number of partitions (2 - 250) to split the report into. If a value is provided for this property, a value must also be provided for the partition property.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.partition
member.summary: The zero-based partition number to retrieve in this export request. If a value is provided for this property, a value must also be provided for the numberOfPartitions property.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.isLocked
member.summary: If true, include only documents that are locked. If false, include only documents that are not locked. Defaults to false if not specified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.merchantSellerIdentifier
member.summary: If set, include only documents associated with these merchantSellerIds.Multiple merchantSellerIds should be sent by comma separated values.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.documentStatus
member.summary: DocumentStatus For documentStatus, accepted values are: Saved, Posted, Committed, Cancelled

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.isModifiedDateSameAsDocumentDate
member.summary: Use this parameter when dateFilter = ModifiedDate. For dateFilter = DocumentDate, PaymentDate, TaxDate or ReportingDate, the isModifiedDateSameAsDocumentDate parameter is ignored. Set this parameter to true when you would like to get Documents which have the Document Date same as Modified Date. Defaults to false if not specified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.taxGroup
member.summary: TaxGroup is required to support Sales tax (Sales + SellersUse) and VAT (Input+ Output). TaxTypes, such as Lodging, Bottle, LandedCost, Ewaste, BevAlc, etc

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.taxName
member.summary: The description of the tax

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.taxCode
member.summary: The AvaTax tax code or customer tax code associated with the item or SKU in the transaction

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.customerVendorCode
member.summary: The code your business application uses to identify a customer or vendor

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.taxSubType
member.summary: Defines the individual taxes associated with a TaxType category, such as Lodging TaxType which supports numerous TaxSubTypes, including Hotel, Occupancy, ConventionCenter, Accommotations, etc.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.reportSource
member.summary: Defines report source.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.compression
member.summary: Defines the compression mode of the result file For compression, the accepted values are: NONE, GZIP

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.includeDocumentLineDetails
member.summary: If true, include document line details in the generated report. If false, include only document line in the generated report. Defaults to false if not specified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.ExportDocumentLineModel.includeMultiTaxLineDetails
member.summary: If true, include multi tax line details in the generated report. If false, include document or document line in the generated report based on includeDocumentLineDetails. Defaults to false if not specified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingCalendarModel.formCountry
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country that issued the tax form for this filing calendar.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingCalendarModel.formRegion
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region that issued the tax form for this filing calendar.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingCalendarModel.taxFormCode
member.summary: The Avalara standard tax form code of the tax form for this filing calendar. The first two characters of the tax form code are the ISO 3166 country code of the country that issued this form.

member.summary: The earliest date for the tax period when this return should be filed. This date specifies the earliest date for tax transactions that should be reported on this filing calendar. member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingModel
member.summary: Represents a listing of all tax calculation data for filings and for accruing to future filings.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this filing.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingModel.companyId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the company for this filing.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingModel.month
member.summary: The month of the filing period for this tax filing. The filing period represents the year and month of the last day of taxes being reported on this filing. For example, an annual tax filing for Jan-Dec 2015 would have a filing period of Dec 2015.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.FilingModel.year
member.summary: The year of the filing period for this tax filing. The filing period represents the year and month of the last day of taxes being reported on this filing. For example, an annual tax filing for Jan-Dec 2015 would have a filing period of Dec 2015.

member.summary: Indicates whether this is an original or an amended filing.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel
member.summary:
Describes an element in the harmonized tariff system.
According to the [United States International Trade Commission](https://www.usitc.gov), the harmonized tariff schedule is defined as follows:
The HTS is a U.S. nomenclature system used to classify traded goods based on their material composition, product name, and/or intended function. The HTS is designed so that each article falls into only one category. It is divided into chapters, each of which has a 2-digit number. Each product category within the various chapters is designated by 4, 6, 8, or 10 digits. The 4-digit categories are called "headings." The 6-, 8- and 10-digit classifications are called "subheadings."
Within AvaTax, the `HsCodeModel` object can refer to sections, chapters, headings, subheadings, or articles. Each object represents one classification. Many of these objects have child objects underneath them; these child objects are more specific than their parent objects.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.hsCode
member.summary:
The harmonized tariff system code for this section and chapter.
A full HS code contains more than six characters. Partial HS codes with two, four, or six characters may have child codes underneath them. A child code is one that contains greater specificity than a parent code. It is recommended that when you identify a product you use the most detailed code available to identify it.
Top level sections do not have HS Codes.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.id
member.summary:
A unique identifier for this harmonized tariff system code.
To search for a list of child codes underneath a specific HS code, search for codes where the child's `parentHsCodeId` value matches the parent's `id` value.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.parentHsCodeId
member.summary:
The unique ID number of the parent HS code or HS code prefix.
To search for a list of child codes underneath a specific HS code, search for codes where the child's `parentHsCodeId` value matches the parent's `id` value.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.description
member.summary: A human readable description that identifies Code descriptive text for this Section, Chapter, Heading, or Subheading.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.system
member.summary: The system to which this HS code belongs.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.destinationCountry
member.summary: The destination country identified with this HS Code. This value applies when certain products are classified in specific ways by bilateral trade agreements.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.effDate
member.summary:
For codes that have been expired or defined on specific dates, this value indicates the earliest date for which this code is considered valid.
If this value is null, this code can be used for any valid date earlier than its end date.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.endDate
member.summary:
For codes that have been expired or defined on specific dates, this value indicates the latest date for which this code is considered valid.
If this value is null, this code can be used for any valid date later than its effective date.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.HsCodeModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.summary:
List of classifications that belong to this item. A single classification consists of a productCode and a systemCode for a particular item.
Please note: `taxCode` (ProductCode for SystemCode `AVATAXCODE`) is being removed from `classifications`. You can still find it in the `taxCode` field.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel
member.summary:
Represents an override of tax jurisdictions for a specific address.
During the time period represented by EffDate through EndDate, all tax decisions for addresses matching this override object will be assigned to the list of jurisdictions designated in this object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.accountId
member.summary: The unique ID number assigned to this account.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.description
member.summary: A description of why this jurisdiction override was created.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.line1
member.summary: The street address of the physical location affected by this override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.city
member.summary: The city address of the physical location affected by this override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country to be affected by this override.
Note that only United States addresses are affected by the jurisdiction override system.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.country
member.summary:
The two character ISO-3166 country code of the country affected by this override.
Note that only United States addresses are affected by the jurisdiction override system.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.postalCode
member.summary: The postal code of the physical location affected by this override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.effectiveDate
member.summary: The date when this override first takes effect. Set this value to null to affect all dates up to the end date.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.endDate
member.summary: The date when this override will cease to take effect. Set this value to null to never expire.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.JurisdictionOverrideModel.createdDate
member.summary: The date when this record was created.

member.summary: The User ID of the user who created this record.

member.summary: The date/time when this record was last modified.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel
member.summary: Represents one line item in a transaction

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.number
member.summary: The line number of this line within the document. This can be any text that is useful to you, such as numeric line numbers, alphabetic line numbers, or other text.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.quantity
member.summary:
Quantity of items in this line. This quantity value should always be a positive value representing the quantity of product that changed hands, even when handling returns or refunds.
If not provided, or if set to zero, the quantity value is assumed to be one (1).

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.amount
member.summary:
Total amount for this line. The amount represents the net currency value that changed hands from the customer (represented by the `customerCode` field) to the company (represented by the `companyCode`) field.
For sale transactions, this value must be positive. It indicates the amount of money paid by the customer to the company.
For refund or return transactions, this value must be negative.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.taxCode
member.summary:
Tax Code - System or Custom Tax Code.
You can use your own tax code mapping or standard Avalara tax codes. For a full list of tax codes, see `ListTaxCodes`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 10/16/2017, Version: 17.11, Message: Please use `entityUseCode` instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.entityUseCode
member.summary:
Entity Use Code - The client application customer or usage type. This field allows you to designate a type of usage that may make this transaction considered exempt by reason of exempt usage.
For a list of entity use codes, see the Definitions API `ListEntityUseCodes`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.itemCode
member.summary: Item Code (SKU). If you provide an `itemCode` field, the AvaTax API will look up the item you created with the `CreateItems` API call and use all the information available about that item for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.exemptionCode
member.summary: The customer Tax Id Number (tax_number) associated with a certificate - Sales tax calculation requests first determine if there is an applicable ECMS entry available, and will utilize it for exemption processing. If no applicable ECMS entry is available, the AvaTax service will determine if an Exemption Number field is populated or an Entity/Use Code is included in the sales tax calculation request, and will perform exemption processing using either of those two options. Note: This is same as 'exemptNo' in TransactionModel.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.discounted
member.summary: True if the document discount should be applied to this line. If this value is false, or not provided, discounts will not be applied to this line even if they are specified on the root `discount` element.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.taxIncluded
member.summary:
Indicates whether the `amount` for this line already includes tax.
If this value is `true`, the final price of this line including tax will equal the value in `amount`.
If this value is `null` or `false`, the final price will equal `amount` plus whatever taxes apply to this line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.revenueAccount
member.summary:
Revenue Account (Customer Defined Field).
This field is available for you to use to provide whatever information your implementation requires. It does not affect tax calculation.

member.summary:
Ref1 (Customer Defined Field)
This field is available for you to use to provide whatever information your implementation requires. It does not affect tax calculation.

member.summary:
Ref2 (Customer Defined Field)
This field is available for you to use to provide whatever information your implementation requires. It does not affect tax calculation.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.description
member.summary:
Item description.
For Streamlined Sales Tax (SST) customers, this field is required if an unmapped `itemCode` is used.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.businessIdentificationNo
member.summary:
VAT business identification number for the customer for this line item. If you leave this field empty, this line item will use whatever business identification number you provided at the transaction level.
If you specify a VAT business identification number for the customer in this transaction and you have also set up a business identification number for your company during company setup, this transaction will be treated as a business-to-business transaction for VAT purposes and it will be calculated according to VAT tax rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.parameters
member.summary:
Special parameters that apply to this line within this transaction.
To get a full list of available parameters, please use the `ListParameters` API.

member.summary: Custom user fields/flex fields for this line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LineItemModel.hsCode
member.summary:
The Item code for Custom Duty / Global Import tax determination Harmonized Tariff System code for this transaction.
For a list of harmonized tariff codes, see the Definitions API for harmonized tariff codes.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LocationModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country of the physical address of this location.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LocationModel.postalCode
member.summary: The postal code or zip code of the physical address of this location.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.LocationModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country of the physical address of this location.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiCompanyLineItemModel.addresses
member.summary:
The addresses to use for this transaction line.
If you set this value to `null`, or if you omit this element from your API call, then instead the transaction will use the `addresses` from the document level.
If you specify any other value besides `null`, only addresses specified for this line will be used for this line.

member.summary:
Tax Code - System or Custom Tax Code.
You can use your own tax code mapping or standard Avalara tax codes. For a full list of tax codes, see `ListTaxCodes`.

member.summary: Indicates if line has Tax Included; defaults to false

member.summary: Revenue Account

member.summary: Reference 1 - Client specific reference field

member.summary: Reference 2 - Client specific reference field

member.summary: Item description. This is required for SST transactions if an unmapped ItemCode is used.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiCompanyLineItemModel.businessIdentificationNo
member.summary:
VAT business identification number for the customer for this line item. If you leave this field empty, this line item will use whatever business identification number you provided at the transaction level.
If you specify a VAT business identification number for the customer in this transaction and you have also set up a business identification number for your company during company setup, this transaction will be treated as a business-to-business transaction for VAT purposes and it will be calculated according to VAT tax rules.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel
member.summary: Represents one line item in a MultiDocument transaction

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.companyCode
member.summary:
Specify the code of the company for this line of transaction.
If you leave this value null, the `companyCode` at the root level will be used instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.reportingLocationCode
member.summary:
Sets the sale location code (Outlet ID) for reporting this document to the tax authority.
If you leave this value `null`, the `reportingLocationCode` at the root level will be used instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.number
member.summary: The line number of this line within the document. This can be any text that is useful to you, such as numeric line numbers, alphabetic line numbers, or other text.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.quantity
member.summary:
Quantity of items in this line. This quantity value should always be a positive value representing the quantity of product that changed hands, even when handling returns or refunds.
If not provided, or if set to zero, the quantity value is assumed to be one (1).

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.amount
member.summary:
Total amount for this line. The amount represents the net currency value that changed hands from the customer (represented by the `customerCode` field) to the company (represented by the `companyCode`) field.
For sale transactions, this value must be positive. It indicates the amount of money paid by the customer to the company.
For refund or return transactions, this value must be negative.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.taxCode
member.summary:
Tax Code - System or Custom Tax Code.
You can use your own tax code mapping or standard Avalara tax codes. For a full list of tax codes, see `ListTaxCodes`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 10/16/2017, Version: 17.11, Message: Please use `entityUseCode` instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.entityUseCode
member.summary:
Entity Use Code - The client application customer or usage type. This field allows you to designate a type of usage that may make this transaction considered exempt by reason of exempt usage.
For a list of entity use codes, see the Definitions API `ListEntityUseCodes`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.itemCode
member.summary: Item Code (SKU). If you provide an `itemCode` field, the AvaTax API will look up the item you created with the `CreateItems` API call and use all the information available about that item for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.exemptionCode
member.summary: The customer Tax Id Number (tax_number) associated with a certificate - Sales tax calculation requests first determine if there is an applicable ECMS entry available, and will utilize it for exemption processing. If no applicable ECMS entry is available, the AvaTax service will determine if an Exemption Number field is populated or an Entity/Use Code is included in the sales tax calculation request, and will perform exemption processing using either of those two options. Note: This is same as 'exemptNo' in TransactionModel.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.discounted
member.summary: True if the document discount should be applied to this line. If this value is false, or not provided, discounts will not be applied to this line even if they are specified on the root `discount` element.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.taxIncluded
member.summary:
Indicates whether the `amount` for this line already includes tax.
If this value is `true`, the final price of this line including tax will equal the value in `amount`.
If this value is `null` or `false`, the final price will equal `amount` plus whatever taxes apply to this line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.revenueAccount
member.summary:
Revenue Account (Customer Defined Field).
This field is available for you to use to provide whatever information your implementation requires. It does not affect tax calculation.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.ref1
member.summary:
Ref1 (Customer Defined Field)
This field is available for you to use to provide whatever information your implementation requires. It does not affect tax calculation.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.ref2
member.summary:
Ref2 (Customer Defined Field)
This field is available for you to use to provide whatever information your implementation requires. It does not affect tax calculation.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.description
member.summary:
Item description.
For Streamlined Sales Tax (SST) customers, this field is required if an unmapped `itemCode` is used.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.businessIdentificationNo
member.summary:
VAT business identification number for the customer for this line item. If you leave this field empty, this line item will use whatever business identification number you provided at the transaction level.
If you specify a VAT business identification number for the customer in this transaction and you have also set up a business identification number for your company during company setup, this transaction will be treated as a business-to-business transaction for VAT purposes and it will be calculated according to VAT tax rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.parameters
member.summary:
Special parameters that apply to this line within this transaction.
To get a full list of available parameters, please use the `ListParameters` API.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.userDefinedFields
member.summary: Custom user fields/flex fields for this line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentLineItemModel.hsCode
member.summary:
The Item code for Custom Duty / Global Import tax determination Harmonized Tariff System code for this transaction.
For a list of harmonized tariff codes, see the Definitions API for harmonized tariff codes.
