member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentModel
member.summary:
A MultiDocument transaction represents a sale or purchase that occurred between more than two companies.
A traditional transaction requires exactly two parties: a seller and a buyer. MultiDocument transactions can involve a marketplace of vendors, each of which contributes some portion of the final transaction. Within a MultiDocument transaction, each individual buyer and seller pair are matched up and converted to a separate document. This separation of documents allows each seller to file their taxes separately.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this MultiDocument object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentModel.accountId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the AvaTax customer account that created this MultiDocument object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentModel.code
member.summary:
The transaction code of the MultiDocument transaction.
All individual transactions within this MultiDocument object will have this code as a prefix.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.MultiDocumentModel.type
member.summary:
Indicates the type of MultiDocument transaction. Common values are:
* SalesOrder - An estimate that is not permanently recorded * SalesInvoice - An invoice that can be reported on a tax return
For more information about document types, see [DocumentType](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/models/enums/DocumentType/)

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel
member.summary: Represents a request for a new account with Avalara for a new subscriber. Contains information about the account requested and the rate plan selected.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.offer
member.summary:
The offer code provided to you by your Avalara business development contact.
This code controls what services and rates the customer will be provisioned with upon creation.
If you do not know your offer code, please contact your Avalara business development representative.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.connectorId
member.summary: The id associated with the connector

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.campaign
member.summary:
If your Avalara business development representative requests, please provide the campaign ID associated with your signup process. This campaign identifier helps Avalara match users to the context in which they learned about the product to help improve the accuracy of our messaging.
The `campaign` field must be either null or a value provided to you by an Avalara business development representative. If you provide an unexpected value in this field, your API call will fail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.leadSource
member.summary:
If your Avalara business development representative requests, please provide the lead source value associated with your signup process. This lead source identifier helps Avalara match users to the context in which they learned about the product to help improve the accuracy of our messaging.
The `leadSource` field must be either null or a value provided to you by an Avalara business development representative. If you provide an unexpected value in this field, your API call will fail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.effectiveDate
member.summary:
The date on which the account should take effect. If null, defaults to today.
You should leave this value `null` unless specifically requested by your Avalara business development manager.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.endDate
member.summary:
The date on which the account should expire.
You should leave this value `null` unless specifically requested by your Avalara business development manager.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.accountName
member.summary: The name of the account to create

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.website
member.summary:
Website of the new customer whose account is being created.
It is strongly recommended to provide the customer's website URL, as this will help our support representatives better assist customers.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.paymentMethodId
member.summary:
Payment Method to be associated with the account.
This is strictly to be used internally unless your Avalara business development manager specifically asks you to provide this value while attempting to create an account.

member.summary: Email of the primary contact person for this account

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.username
member.summary: The username to be associated with the user created. If this is not provided, email address will be used as the username.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.userPassword
member.summary:
If instructed by your Avalara business development manager, set this value to a temporary password to permit the user to continue their onboarding process.
If this value is null, a temporary password is generated by the system and emailed to the user.
The user will then be asked to choose a permanent password when they first log on to the AvaTax website.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.welcomeEmail
member.summary:
This option controls what type of a welcome email is sent when the account is created.
* `Normal` - A standard welcome email will be sent. * `Suppressed` - No email will be sent. * `Custom` - If your Avalara business development representative provides you with a customized welcome email for your customers, please select this option.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.companyCode
member.summary:
Company code to be assigned to the company created for this account.
If no company code is provided, this will be defaulted to "DEFAULT" company code.

member.summary:
Set this to true if and only if the owner of the newly created account accepts Avalara's terms and conditions for your account.
Reading and accepting Avalara's terms and conditions is necessary in order for the account to receive a license key.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NewAccountRequestModel.haveReadAvalaraTermsAndConditions
member.summary:
Set this to true if and only if the owner of the newly created account has fully read Avalara's terms and conditions for your account.
Reading and accepting Avalara's terms and conditions is necessary in order for the account to receive a license key.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel
member.summary:
Represents a declaration of nexus within a particular taxing jurisdiction.
To create a nexus declaration for your company, you must first call the Definitions API `ListNexus` to obtain a list of Avalara-defined nexus. Once you have determined which nexus you wish to declare, you should customize only the user-selectable fields in this object.
The user selectable fields for the nexus object are `companyId`, `effectiveDate`, `endDate`, `localNexusTypeId`, `taxId`, `nexusTypeId`, `hasPermanentEstablishment`, and `isSellerImporterOfRecord`.
When calling `CreateNexus` or `UpdateNexus`, all values in your nexus object except for the user-selectable fields must match an Avalara-defined system nexus object. You can retrieve a list of Avalara-defined system nexus objects by calling `ListNexus`. If any data does not match, AvaTax may not recognize your nexus declaration.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.id
member.summary:
The unique ID number of this declaration of nexus.
This field is defined automatically when you declare nexus. You do not need to provide a value for this field.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.companyId
member.summary:
The unique ID number of the company that declared nexus.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country in which this company declared nexus.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.jurisTypeId
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 12/20/2017, Version: 18.1, Message: Please use jurisdictionTypeId instead. The jurisdiction type of the jurisdiction in which this company declared nexus.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.jurisdictionTypeId
member.summary:
The type of the jurisdiction in which this company declared nexus.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.jurisCode
member.summary:
The code identifying the jurisdiction in which this company declared nexus.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.jurisName
member.summary:
The common name of the jurisdiction in which this company declared nexus.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.effectiveDate
member.summary:
The date when this nexus began. If not known, set to null.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.endDate
member.summary:
If this nexus will end or has ended on a specific date, set this to the date when this nexus ends.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.shortName
member.summary:
The short name of the jurisdiction.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.signatureCode
member.summary:
The signature code of the boundary region as defined by Avalara.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.stateAssignedNo
member.summary:
The state assigned number of this jurisdiction.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.nexusTypeId
member.summary:
The type of nexus that this company is declaring.
If you are voluntarily declaring nexus in a jurisdiction, you should select `SalesOrSellersUseTax` for your nexus type option. This option allows you to calculate tax correctly whether you are selling in-state or shipping from an out-of-state location.
If you are legally obligated to declare nexus due to physical presence or other sufficient nexus, you should select `SalesTax`. This indicates that, as a legal requirement, your company must always collect and remit full sales tax in this jurisdiction.
If you are participating in the Streamlined Sales Tax program, your SST administrator will select nexus settings for you in all SST jurisdictions. Do not select any SST options by yourself.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.sourcing
member.summary:
Indicates whether this nexus is defined as origin or destination nexus.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.hasLocalNexus
member.summary:
True if you are also declaring local nexus within this jurisdiction. Many U.S. states have options for declaring nexus in local jurisdictions as well as within the state.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.localNexusTypeId
member.summary:
If you are declaring local nexus within this jurisdiction, this indicates whether you are declaring only a specified list of local jurisdictions, all state-administered local jurisdictions, or all local jurisdictions.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.hasPermanentEstablishment
member.summary:
Set this value to true if your company has a permanent establishment within this jurisdiction.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.taxId
member.summary:
Optional - the tax identification number under which you declared nexus.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.streamlinedSalesTax
member.summary:
DEPRECATED - Date: 4/29/2017, Version: 19.4, Message: Please use isSSTActive instead. For the United States, this flag indicates whether this particular nexus falls within a U.S. State that participates in the Streamlined Sales Tax program. For countries other than the US, this flag is null.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.isSSTActive
member.summary:
For the United States, this flag indicates whether this particular nexus falls within a U.S. State that participates in the Streamlined Sales Tax program and if the account associated with the Nexus has an active AvaTaxCsp subscription. For countries other than the US, this flag is null.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.createdDate
member.summary:
The date when this record was created.
This field is defined automatically when you declare nexus. You do not need to provide a value for this field.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.createdUserId
member.summary:
The User ID of the user who created this record.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.modifiedDate

The date/time when this record was last modified.

The user ID of the user who last modified this record.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.taxTypeGroup
member.summary:
The type group of nexus that this company is declaring Use [ListTaxTypeGroups](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListTaxTypeGroups/) API for a list of nexus tax type groups.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`. NOTE: This optional field will trigger nexus subtype lookup when populated. When using make sure TaxTypeGroup matches corresponding NexusTaxTypeGroup

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.nexusTaxTypeGroup
member.summary:
The type of nexus that this company is declaring.Replaces NexusTypeId. Use [ListNexusTaxTypeGroups](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListNexusTaxTypeGroups/) API for a list of nexus tax type groups.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.taxAuthorityId
member.summary:
A unique ID number of the tax authority that is associated with this nexus.
This field is defined by Avalara. All Avalara-defined fields must match an Avalara-defined nexus object found by calling `ListNexus`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.isSellerImporterOfRecord
member.summary:
For nexus declarations at the country level, specifies whether this company is considered the importer of record in this nexus region.
Some taxes only apply if the seller is the importer of record for a product. In cases where companies are working together to ship products, there may be mutual agreement as to which company is the entity designated as importer of record. The importer of record will then be the company designated to pay taxes marked as being obligated to the importer of record.
Set this value to `true` to consider your company as the importer of record and collect these taxes. Leave this value as false or null and taxes will be calculated as if your company is not the importer of record.
This value may also be set during each transaction API call. See `CreateTransaction()` for more information.
This field is user-selectable and should be provided when creating or updating a nexus object.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NexusModel.taxName
member.summary:
A description of corresponding tax type applied to the nexus.
When a custom nexus is created, it'll have to be matched to a system nexus to be validated successfully. The matched system nexus has a field to describe the tax type applied to it, that field will be copied over to the nexus that is being created.
This field is defined by Avalara. Its main purpose is to give a simple description of the tax type associated with the nexus so users have a better understanding of the nexus when it is displayed.

member.summary: NoticeCustomerTypeID can be retrieved from the definitions API

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NoticeModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country that sent this notice.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NoticeModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region within the country that sent this notice.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel
member.summary:
Represents a single notification.
A notification is a message from Avalara that may have relevance to your business. You may want to regularly review notifications and then dismiss them when you are certain that you have addressed any relevant concerns raised by this notification.
An example of a notification would be a message about new software, or a change to AvaTax that may affect you, or a potential issue with your company's tax profile.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.id
member.summary: The unique id of the notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.accountId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the account that received this notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.companyId
member.summary: If this notification was tied to a specific company, this will be the unique ID number of the company that received the notification. Notifications that are tied to accounts will have a `NULL` value for `companyId`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.referenceObject
member.summary:
The type name of object referred to by this notification, if any.
Some notifications may include information about a related data object.
For example, if this notification was related to a nexus declaration, the `referenceObject` field would be `Nexus` and the `referenceId` field would be the unique ID number of that nexus.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.referenceId
member.summary:
The unique reference Id number of the object referred to by this notification, if any.
Some notifications may include information about a related data object.
For example, if this notification was related to a nexus declaration, the `referenceObject` field would be `Nexus` and the `referenceId` field would be the unique ID number of that nexus.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.severityLevelId
member.summary: The severity level of the notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.category
member.summary:
The category of this notification.
Notification categories are a useful way to group related notifications together. Category names may change over time.
For Example: "Backdated Transactions" or "Nexus Jurisdiction Alerts", or "Certificate Expiration".

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.topic
member.summary:
The topic of this notification.
Notification topics contain information about the notification. They are a summary of the issue and can help you decide what type of action to take.
For Example: "Backdated Transactions" or "Nexus Jurisdiction Alerts", or "Certificate Expiration".

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.message
member.summary: The message for this notification. This is a friendly description of the notification and any relevant information that can help you decide what kind of action, if any, to take in response.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.needsAction
member.summary: If this notification object requires user action to resolve, this value will be set to true.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.actionName
member.summary:
If there is a specific action suggested by this notification, this is the name of the action.
An action is a suggested next step such as "Review Your Tax Profile." If an action is suggested, you should give the viewer a hyperlink to the location referred to by `actionLink` and give the hyperlink the name `actionName`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.actionLink
member.summary:
If there is a specific action suggested by this notification, this is the URL of the action.
An action is a suggested next step such as "Review Your Tax Profile." If an action is suggested, you should give the viewer a hyperlink to the location referred to by `actionLink` and give the hyperlink the name `actionName`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.actionDueDate
member.summary:
If there is a specific action suggested by this notification, and if this action is requested by a specific due date, this value will be the due date for the action.
An action is a suggested next step such as "Review Your Tax Profile." If an action is suggested, you should give the viewer a hyperlink to the location referred to by `actionLink` and give the hyperlink the name `actionName`.
For actions that have deadlines, such as "Confirm your tax registration before filing", this value will be set to the deadline date for the action. Otherwise, this value will be null.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.dismissed
member.summary: When a user has finished reviewing a notification, they may opt to dismiss it by calling the `DismissNotification` API. This API marks the notification as dismissed, and dismissed notifications will generally not appear in most user interfaces.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.dismissedByUserId
member.summary: If this notification has been dismissed, this indicates the unique ID number of the user that dismissed the notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.dismissedDate
member.summary: If this notification has been dismissed, this indicates the timestamp (in UTC time) when the user dismissed the notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.expireDate
member.summary: If this notification is time sensitive, this is the latest date when the notification should be displayed to the user.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.createdUserId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the user who created the notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.createdDate
member.summary: The UTC timestamp of the time when this notification was created.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.modifiedUserId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the user who most recently modified this notification.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.NotificationModel.modifiedDate
member.summary: The UTC timestamp of the time when this notification was last modified.

member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel
member.summary:
A preferred program is a customs and/or duty program that can be used to handle cross-border transactions. Customers who sign up for a preferred program may obtain better terms for their customs and duty payments.
To indicate that your company has signed up for a preferred program, specify the `code` value from this object as the value for the `AvaTax.LC.PreferredProgram` parameter in your transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number representing this preferred program.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.code
member.summary: A code that identifies this preferred program. To select this program, specify this code value in the `AvaTax.LC.PreferredProgram` parameter.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.originCountry
member.summary: The ISO 3166 country code for the origin permitted by this program

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.destinationCountry
member.summary: The ISO 3166 country code for the destination permitted by this program

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.effectiveDate
member.summary: The earliest date for which this preferred program can be used in AvaTax. If `null`, this preferred program is valid for all dates earlier than `endDate`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.endDate
member.summary: The latest date for which this preferred program can be used in AvaTax. If `null`, this preferred program is valid for all dates later than `effectiveDate`.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.PreferredProgramModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel
member.summary: Refund a committed transaction

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.refundTransactionCode
member.summary: The transaction code for the refund. This code will be saved to the `ReturnInvoice` transaction, and does not need to match the code of the original sale.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.refundDate
member.summary: The date of the refund. For customers using Avalara's Managed Returns Service, this date controls the month in which the refund transaction will be reported on a tax filing.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.refundType
member.summary:
Type of this refund.
To submit a full refund, specify `Full`.
To refund only specific lines from the transaction, specify `Partial` and indicate the lines you wish to apply in the `refundLines` field.
To refund the tax that was paid in the previous transaction, specify `TaxOnly`.
To issue a percentage-based discount, specify `Percentage`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.refundPercentage
member.summary:
The percentage for refund.
This value only applies if you choose `refundType = Percentage` or `refundType = Partial`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.refundLines
member.summary: If you chose a refund of type `Partial`, this indicates which lines from the original transaction are being refunded.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.referenceCode
member.summary: A user-defined reference field containing information about this refund.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.RefundTransactionModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel
member.summary:
This object is used to keep track of custom information about a company.
The company settings system is a metadata system that you can use to store extra information about a company. Your integration or connector could use this data storage to keep track of preference information, reminders, or any other storage that would need to persist even if the customer uninstalls your application.
A setting can refer to any type of data you need to remember about this company object. When creating this object, you may define your own `set`, `name`, and `value` parameters. To define your own values, please choose a `set` name that begins with `X-` to indicate an extension.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this setting.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.companyId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the company this setting refers to.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.set
member.summary:
A user-defined "set" containing this setting.
Avalara defines some sets that cannot be changed. To create your own set, choose a set name that begins with `X-` to indicate that this is an extension value.
We recommend that you choose a set name that clearly identifies your application, and then store data within name/value pairs within that set. For example, if you were creating an application called MyApp, you might choose to create a set named `X-MyCompany-MyApp`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.name
member.summary: A user-defined "name" for this name-value pair.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.value
member.summary: The value of this name-value pair.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.modifiedDate
member.summary: The value when the entry was last modified.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.modifiedUserId
member.summary: The value identifying who last modified the entry.

member.name: M:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettingModel.ToString
member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.SettleTransactionModel
member.summary:
Settle this transaction with your ledger by executing one or many actions against that transaction.
You may use this endpoint to verify the transaction, change the transaction's code, and commit the transaction for reporting purposes. This endpoint may be used to execute any or all of these actions at once.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxOverrideModel.taxAmount
member.summary:
Indicates a total override of the calculated tax on the document. AvaTax will distribute the override across all the lines.
Tax will be distributed on a best effort basis. It may not always be possible to override all taxes. Please consult your account manager for information about overrides.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxOverrideModel.taxDate
member.summary:
The override tax date to use
This is used when the tax has been previously calculated as in the case of a layaway, return or other reason indicated by the Reason element. If the date is not overridden, then it should be set to the same as the DocDate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxOverrideModel.reason
member.summary:
This provides the reason for a tax override for audit purposes. It is required for types 2-4.
Typical reasons include: "Return" "Layaway"

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxOverrideModel.taxAmountByTaxTypes
member.summary:
Indicates a total override of the calculated tax on the line with TaxType. AvaTax will distribute the override across all the line details for that TaxType.
TaxAmountByTaxType can be used only at the Line level.

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRateModel
member.summary:
Contains information about the general tangible personal property sales tax rates for this jurisdiction.
This rate is calculated by making assumptions about the tax calculation process. It does not account for:
* Sourcing rules, such as origin-and-destination based transactions. * Product taxability rules, such as different tax rates for different product types. * Nexus declarations, where some customers are not obligated to collect tax in specific jurisdictions. * Tax thresholds and rate differences by amounts. * And many more custom use cases.
To upgrade to a fully-featured and accurate tax process that handles these scenarios correctly, please contact Avalara to upgrade to AvaTax!

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel
member.summary:
Represents a tax rule that changes the behavior of Avalara's tax engine for certain products and/or entity use codes in certain jurisdictions.
Avalara supports a few different types of tax rules. For information about tax rule types, see [TaxRuleTypeId](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/models/enums/TaxRuleTypeId/)
Because different types of tax rules have different behavior, some fields may change their behavior based on the type of tax rule selected. Please read the documentation for each field carefully and ensure that the value you send is appropriate for the type of tax rule.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this tax rule.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.companyId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the company that owns this tax rule.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxCodeId
member.summary:
For rules that apply to a specific tax code only, this specifies which tax code is affected by this rule.
You can choose to specify a tax code either by passing its unique ID number in the `taxCodeId` field or by passing its alphanumeric code in the `taxCode` field. To search for the appropriate tax code for your custom rule, use the `ListTaxCodes` API.
The `RateOverrideRule`, `BaseRule`, and `ExemptEntityRule` rule types can be applied to all tax codes. To make a rule that applies to all tax codes, leave both fields blank.
The `ProductTaxabilityRule` rule must be associated with a tax code. If you attempt to create a product taxability rule without a tax code, you will get an error message.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxCode
member.summary:
For rules that apply to a specific tax code only, this specifies which tax code is affected by this rule.
You can choose to specify a tax code either by passing its unique ID number in the `taxCodeId` field or by passing its alphanumeric code in the `taxCode` field. To search for the appropriate tax code for your custom rule, use the `ListTaxCodes` API.
The `RateOverrideRule`, `BaseRule`, and `ExemptEntityRule` rule types can be applied to all tax codes. To make a rule that applies to all tax codes, leave both fields blank.
The `ProductTaxabilityRule` rule must be associated with a tax code. If you attempt to create a product taxability rule without a tax code, you will get an error message.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.stateFIPS
member.summary:
For U.S. tax rules, this is the state's Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) code.
This field is required for rules that apply to specific jurisdictions in the United States. It is not required if you set the `isAllJuris` flag to true.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.jurisName
member.summary:
The name of the jurisdiction to which this tax rule applies.
Custom tax rules can apply to a specific jurisdiction or to all jurisdictions. To select a jurisdiction, use the [ListJurisdictions API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListJurisdictions/) or the [ListJurisdictionsByAddress API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListJurisdictionsByAddress/). To set a rule that applies to all jurisdictions of a specific type, see `isAllJuris`.
Once you have determined which jurisdiction you wish to assign to the tax rule, you should fill in the `jurisName`, `jurisCode`, and `jurisdictionTypeId` fields using the information you retrieved from the API above.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.jurisCode
member.summary:
The code of the jurisdiction to which this tax rule applies.
Custom tax rules can apply to a specific jurisdiction or to all jurisdictions. To select a jurisdiction, use the [ListJurisdictions API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListJurisdictions/) or the [ListJurisdictionsByAddress API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListJurisdictionsByAddress/).
Once you have determined which jurisdiction you wish to assign to the tax rule, you should fill in the `jurisName`, `jurisCode`, and `jurisdictionTypeId` fields using the information you retrieved from the API above.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.jurisTypeId
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 12/20/2017, Version: 18.1, Message: Please use `jurisdictionTypeId` instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.jurisdictionTypeId
member.summary:
The type of the jurisdiction to which this tax rule applies.
Custom tax rules can apply to a specific jurisdiction or to all jurisdictions. To select a jurisdiction, use the [ListJurisdictions API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListJurisdictions/) or the [ListJurisdictionsByAddress API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListJurisdictionsByAddress/).
Once you have determined which jurisdiction you wish to assign to the tax rule, you should fill in the `jurisName`, `jurisCode`, and `jurisdictionTypeId` fields using the information you retrieved from the API above.
To make a custom tax rule for US or Canada that applies to all jurisdictions of a specific type, see the `isAllJuris` field for more information.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 10/16/2017, Version: 17.11, Message: Please use `entityUseCode` instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.entityUseCode
member.summary:
The entity use code to which this rule applies.
You can create custom `entityUseCode` values with specific behavior using this API, or you can change the behavior of Avalara's system-defined entity use codes.
For a full list of Avalara-defined entity use codes, see the [ListEntityUseCodes API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListEntityUseCodes/).

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxTypeId
member.summary:
DEPRECATED - Date: 09/30/2021, Version: 21.9.0, Message: Please use `taxTypeCode` instead. Some tax type groups contain multiple different types of tax. To create a rule that affects only one type of tax within a tax type group, set this value to the code matching the specific tax type within that group. The custom tax rule will then only apply to taxes calculated for that specific type.
For rules that affect all tax types, use the value `A` to match `All` tax types within that group.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxTypeCode
member.summary: Indicates the code of the tax type that applies to this rule. Use /api/v2/definitions/taxtypes endpoint to retrieve the list of tax types applicable for your company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxRuleProductDetail
member.summary: TaxRule Product Detail indicates the HSCode(s) to which the tax rule applies.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.rateTypeId
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 8/27/2018, Version: 18.9, Message: Please use `rateTypeCode`, `taxTypeGroup` and `subTaxType` instead.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.rateTypeCode
member.summary:
Indicates the code of the rate type that applies to this rule. Use [ListRateTypesByCountry](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListRateTypesByCountry/) API for a full list of rate type codes.
If you specify a value in the rateTypeCode field, this rule will cause tax lines that are affected by the rule to change to a different rate type code.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxRuleTypeId
member.summary:
This type value determines the behavior of the tax rule.
You can specify that this rule controls the product's taxability or exempt / nontaxable status, the product's rate (for example, if you have been granted an official ruling for your product's rate that differs from the official rate), or other types of behavior.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.isAllJuris
member.summary:
Allows you to make tax rules apply to lower jurisdictions. This feature is only available in the United States and Canada.
* In the United States, this value can be used for rules written at the `State` jurisdictional level. If set to `true`, this rule will at the state level, county level, city level, and special jurisdiction level. * In Canada, this value can be used for rules written at the `Country` or `State` jurisdictional levels. If set to `true`, this rule will at all lower jurisdictional levels.
For any other use case, this value must be `false`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.value
member.summary:
This field has different behavior based on the type of the tax rule.
* For a product taxability rule, this value is either 1 or 0, indicating taxable or non-taxable. * For a rate override rule, this value is the corrected rate stored as a decimal, for example, a rate of 5% would be stored as 0.05 decimal. If you use the special value of 1.0, only the cap and threshold values will be applied and the rate will be left alone.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.cap
member.summary:
The maximum cap for the price of this item according to this rule. Any amount above this cap will not be subject to this rule.
For example, if you must pay 5% of a product's value up to a maximum value of $1000, you would set the `cap` to `1000.00` and the `value` to `0.05`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.threshold
member.summary:
The per-unit threshold that must be met before this rule applies.
For example, if your product is nontaxable unless it is above $100 per product, you would set the `threshold` value to `100`. In this case, the rate for the rule would apply to the entire amount above $100.
You can also create rules that make the entire product taxable if it exceeds a threshold, but is nontaxable if it is below the threshold. To choose this, set the `options` field to the value `TaxAll`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.options
member.summary:
Supports custom options for your tax rule.
Supported options include: * `TaxAll` - This value indicates that the entire amount of the line becomes taxable when the line amount exceeds the `threshold`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.effectiveDate
member.summary: The first date at which this rule applies. If `null`, this rule will apply to all dates prior to the end date.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.endDate
member.summary: The last date for which this rule applies. If `null`, this rule will apply to all dates after the effective date.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.description
member.summary: A friendly name for this tax rule.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.countyFIPS
member.summary:
For U.S. tax rules, this is the county's Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) code.
This field is required for rules that apply to specific jurisdictions in the United States. It is not required if you set the `isAllJuris` flag to true.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.isSTPro
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 8/27/2018, Version: 18.9, Message: This field is no longer required.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.country
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the country where this rule will apply.
This field supports many different country identifiers: * Two character ISO 3166 codes * Three character ISO 3166 codes * Fully spelled out names of the country in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many countries
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListCountries`.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.region
member.summary:
Name or ISO 3166 code identifying the region where this rule will apply.
This field supports many different region identifiers: * Two and three character ISO 3166 region codes * Fully spelled out names of the region in ISO supported languages * Common alternative spellings for many regions
For a full list of all supported codes and names, please see the Definitions API `ListRegions`. NOTE: Region is required for US and not required for non-US countries because the user may be either creating a Country-level or Region-level rule.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.sourcing
member.summary: The sourcing types to which this rule applies.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxTypeGroup
member.summary:
This field has different behavior based on the type of rule.
* For a product taxability rule, if the rule applies to an item, this value will override the tax type group of the original product. * For other rules, this value determines what tax type groups will be affected by the rule.
Refer to `ListTaxTypeGroups` for a list of tax type groups supported by AvaTax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.taxSubType
member.summary:
This field has different behavior based on the type of rule.
* For a product taxability rule, if the rule applies to an item, this value will override the tax sub type of the original product. * For other rules, this value determines what tax sub types will be affected by the rule.
Refer to `ListTaxSubtypes` for a list of tax sub types supported by AvaTax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.nonPassthroughExpression
member.summary: Reserved for Avalara internal usage. Leave this field null.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.currencyCode
member.summary:
The currency code to use for this rule.
For a list of currencies supported by AvaTax, use the [ListCurrencies API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListCurrencies/).

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.preferredProgramId
member.summary: Reserved for Avalara internal usage. Leave this field null.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TaxRuleModel.uomId
member.summary:
For tax rules that are calculated using units of measurement, this indicates the unit of measurement type used to calculate the amounts for this rule.
For a list of units of measurement, use the [ListUnitsOfMeasurement API](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListUnitOfMeasurement/).

member.summary: The two character ISO 3166 country code of the country where this tax detail is assigned.

member.summary: True if this detail element represented an in-state transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.jurisCode
member.summary: The code of the jurisdiction to which this tax detail applies.

member.summary: The name of the jurisdiction to which this tax detail applies.

member.summary: The unique ID number of the jurisdiction to which this tax detail applies.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.stateAssignedNo
member.summary: The state assigned number of the jurisdiction to which this tax detail applies.

member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 12/20/2017, Version: 18.1, Message: Use jurisdictionTypeId instead. The type of the jurisdiction to which this tax detail applies.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.jurisdictionType
member.summary: The type of the jurisdiction in which this tax detail applies.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.nonTaxableAmount
member.summary: The amount of this line item that was considered nontaxable in this tax detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.nonTaxableRuleId
member.summary: The rule according to which portion of this detail was considered nontaxable.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.nonTaxableType
member.summary: The type of nontaxability that was applied to this tax detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.rate
member.summary: The rate at which this tax detail was calculated.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.rateRuleId

member.summary: For Streamlined Sales Tax customers, the SST Electronic Return code under which this tax detail should be applied.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.sourcing
member.summary: Indicates whether this tax detail applies to the origin or destination of the transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.tax
member.summary: The amount of tax for this tax detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxableAmount
member.summary: The taxable amount of this tax detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxType
member.summary: The type of tax that was calculated. Depends on the company's nexus settings as well as the jurisdiction's tax laws.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxSubTypeId
member.summary: The id of the tax subtype.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxTypeGroupId
member.summary: The id of the tax type group.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxName
member.summary: The name of the tax against which this tax amount was calculated. Note: This output field is informational, and the values in this field may change. As such, we discourage hardcoding any string matching on this field.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxAuthorityTypeId
member.summary: The type of the tax authority to which this tax will be remitted.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxRegionId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the tax region.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxCalculated
member.summary: The amount of tax that AvaTax calculated. If an override for tax amount is used, there may be a difference between the tax field which applies your override, and the this amount that is calculated without override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxOverride
member.summary: The amount of tax override that was specified for this tax line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.rateType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 12/20/2017, Version: 18.1, Message: Please use rateTypeCode instead. The rate type for this tax detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.rateTypeCode
member.summary: Indicates the code of the rate type that was used to calculate this tax detail. Use [ListRateTypesByCountry](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/methods/Definitions/ListRateTypesByCountry/) API for a full list of rate type codes.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.taxableUnits
member.summary: Number of units in this line item that were calculated to be taxable according to this rate detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.nonTaxableUnits
member.summary: Number of units in this line item that were calculated to be nontaxable according to this rate detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.exemptUnits
member.summary: Number of units in this line item that were calculated to be exempt according to this rate detail.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.unitOfBasis
member.summary: When calculating units, what basis of measurement did we use for calculating the units?

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.isNonPassThru
member.summary:
True if this value is a non-passthrough tax.
A non-passthrough tax is a tax that may not be charged to a customer; it must be paid directly by the company.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.isFee
member.summary: The Taxes/Fee component. True if the fee is applied. When `isFee` is `false`, this represents a tax rate percentage. For example, `"rate": 0.022500` represents a 2.25% tax rate. When `isFee` is `true`, this represents a specific monetary amount. For example, for `"currencyCode": "USD"` and `"isFee": true`, the `"rate": 15` represents a fee of $15.00 USD.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.reportingTaxableUnits
member.summary: Number of units in this line item that were calculated to be taxable according to this rate detail in the reporting currency.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.reportingNonTaxableUnits
member.summary: Number of units in this line item that were calculated to be nontaxable according to this rate detail in the reporting currency.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.reportingExemptUnits
member.summary: Number of units in this line item that were calculated to be exempt according to this rate detail in the reporting currency.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.reportingTax
member.summary: The amount of tax for this tax detail in the reporting currency.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.reportingTaxCalculated
member.summary: The amount of tax that AvaTax calculated in the reporting currency. If an override for tax amount is used, there may be a difference between the tax field which applies your override, and the this amount that is calculated without override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.liabilityType
member.summary: LiabilityType identifies the party liable to file the tax. This field is used to filter taxes from reports and tax filings as appropriate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.chargedTo
member.summary: ChargedTo identifies the party responsible for covering the tax. This field is used to filter taxes from reports and tax filings as appropriate.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.avtUserBIN
member.summary: ID of the AvaTax user creating the transaction. This field will be calculated by AvaTax based on the Company settings and the transaction details.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.recoverabilityPercentage
member.summary: The percentage of input VAT/GST that is recoverable.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.recoverableAmount
member.summary: The amount of input VAT/GST that is recoverable based on the recoverability percentage.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineDetailModel.nonRecoverableAmount
member.summary: The amount of input VAT/GST that is not recoverable.

member.summary: Convert this object to a JSON string of itself
member.returns: A JSON string of this object

member.name: T:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel
member.summary: One line item on this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.id
member.summary: The unique ID number of this transaction line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.transactionId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the transaction to which this line item belongs.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.lineNumber
member.summary: The line number or code indicating the line on this invoice or receipt or document.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.boundaryOverrideId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the boundary override applied to this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 10/16/2017, Version: 17.11, Message: Please use entityUseCode instead. The customer usage type for this line item. Usage type often affects taxability rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.entityUseCode
member.summary: The entity use code for this line item. Usage type often affects taxability rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.description
member.summary: A description of the item or service represented by this line.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.destinationAddressId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the destination address where this line was delivered or sold. In the case of a point-of-sale transaction, the destination address and origin address will be the same. In the case of a shipped transaction, they will be different.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.originAddressId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the origin address where this line was delivered or sold. In the case of a point-of-sale transaction, the origin address and destination address will be the same. In the case of a shipped transaction, they will be different.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.discountAmount
member.summary: The amount of discount that was applied to this line item. This represents the difference between list price and sale price of the item. In general, a discount represents money that did not change hands; tax is calculated on only the amount of money that changed hands.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.discountTypeId
member.summary: The type of discount, if any, that was applied to this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.exemptAmount
member.summary: The amount of this line item that was exempt.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.exemptCertId
member.summary: The unique ID number of the exemption certificate that applied to this line item. It is the calc_id associated with a certificate in CertCapture.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.certificateId
member.summary: The CertCapture Certificate ID

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.exemptNo
member.summary: The customer Tax Id Number (tax_number) associated with a certificate - Sales tax calculation requests first determine if there is an applicable ECMS entry available, and will utilize it for exemption processing. If no applicable ECMS entry is available, the AvaTax service will determine if an Exemption Number field is populated or an Entity/Use Code is included in the sales tax calculation request, and will perform exemption processing using either of those two options.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.isItemTaxable
member.summary: True if this item is taxable.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.isSSTP
member.summary: True if this item is a Streamlined Sales Tax line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.itemCode
member.summary: The code string of the item represented by this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.lineAmount
member.summary: The total amount of the transaction, including both taxable and exempt. This is the total price for all items. To determine the individual item price, divide this by quantity.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.quantity
member.summary: The quantity of products sold on this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.ref1
member.summary: A user-defined reference identifier for this transaction line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.ref2
member.summary: A user-defined reference identifier for this transaction line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.reportingDate
member.summary: The date when this transaction should be reported. By default, all transactions are reported on the date when the actual transaction took place. In some cases, line items may be reported later due to delayed shipments or other business reasons.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.revAccount
member.summary: The revenue account number for this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.sourcing
member.summary: Indicates whether this line item was taxed according to the origin or destination.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.tax
member.summary:
The tax for this line in this transaction.
If you used a `taxOverride` of type `taxAmount` for this line, this value will represent the amount of your override. AvaTax will still attempt to calculate the correct tax for this line and will store that calculated value in the `taxCalculated` field.
You can compare the `tax` and `taxCalculated` fields to check for any discrepancies between an external tax calculation provider and the calculation performed by AvaTax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxableAmount
member.summary: The taxable amount of this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxCalculated
member.summary:
The amount of tax that AvaTax calculated for the transaction.
If you used a `taxOverride` of type `taxAmount`, there may be a difference between the `tax` field which applies your override, and the `taxCalculated` field which represents the amount of tax that AvaTax calculated without the override.
You can compare the `tax` and `taxCalculated` fields to check for any discrepancies between an external tax calculation provider and the calculation performed by AvaTax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxCode
member.summary: The code string for the tax code that was used to calculate this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxCodeId
member.summary: The unique ID number for the tax code that was used to calculate this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxDate
member.summary: The date that was used for calculating tax amounts for this line item. By default, this date should be the same as the document date. In some cases, for example when a consumer returns a product purchased previously, line items may be calculated using a tax date in the past so that the consumer can receive a refund for the correct tax amount that was charged when the item was originally purchased.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxEngine
member.summary: The tax engine identifier that was used to calculate this line item.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxOverrideType
member.summary: If a tax override was specified, this indicates the type of tax override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.businessIdentificationNo
member.summary: VAT business identification number used for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxOverrideAmount
member.summary: If a tax override was specified, this indicates the amount of tax that was requested.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxOverrideReason
member.summary: If a tax override was specified, represents the reason for the tax override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.taxIncluded
member.summary:
Indicates whether the `amount` for this line already includes tax.
If this value is `true`, the final price of this line including tax will equal the value in `amount`.
If this value is `null` or `false`, the final price will equal `amount` plus whatever taxes apply to this line.

member.summary: ID of the merchant selling on the Marketplace. This field must be populated by Marketplace.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.marketplaceLiabilityType
member.summary: This field will identify who is remitting Marketplace or Seller. This field must be populated by Marketplace.

member.summary: The transaction's original ID in its origination system

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.originationSite
member.summary: Synonym of Marketplace Origination. Name of the Marketplace where the transaction originated from.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.details
member.summary:
Optional: A list of tax details for this line item.
Tax details represent taxes being charged by various tax authorities. Taxes that appear in the `details` collection are intended to be displayed to the customer and charged as a 'tax' on the invoice.
To fetch this list, add the query string `?$include=Details` to your URL.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.accountPayableSalesTaxDetails
member.summary:
Optional: A list of Account payable Sales tax details for this line item.
To fetch this list, add the query string `?$include=AccountPayableSalesTaxDetails` to your URL.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.nonPassthroughDetails
member.summary:
Optional: A list of non-passthrough tax details for this line item.
Tax details represent taxes being charged by various tax authorities. Taxes that appear in the `nonPassthroughDetails` collection are taxes that must be paid directly by the company and not shown to the customer.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionLineModel.lineLocationTypes
member.summary: Optional: A list of location types for this line item. To fetch this list, add the query string "?$include=LineLocationTypes" to your URL.

member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 07/25/2018, Version: 18.7, Message: This field is deprecated and will return null till its removed. The date when payment was made on this transaction. By default, this should be the same as the date of the transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.status
member.summary: The status of the transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.type
member.summary:
The type of the transaction.
Transactions of type `SalesOrder`, `ReturnOrder`, and so on are temporary estimates and will not be saved.
Transactions of type `SalesInvoice, `ReturnInvoice`, and so on are permanent transactions that can be reported to tax authorities if they are in status `Committed`.
A sales transaction represents a sale from the company to a customer. A purchase transaction represents a purchase made by the company. A return transaction represents a customer who decided to request a refund after purchasing a product from the company. An inventory transfer transaction represents goods that were moved from one location of the company to another location without changing ownership.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.batchCode
member.summary: If this transaction was created as part of a batch, this code indicates which batch.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.currencyCode
member.summary: The three-character ISO 4217 currency code representing the ‘invoice currency’ (the currency the transaction was invoiced in). If this is different than the currency the tax liability needs to be reported in, you’ll also need to provide the `exchangeRateCurrencyCode` and the `exchangeRate` for conversion to the reporting country.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.exchangeRateCurrencyCode
member.summary: The three-character ISO 4217 currency code representing the ‘reporting currency’ (the currency the transaction’s tax liability needs to be reported in). You can leave this blank if the invoice currency provided in the `currencyCode` field is also the reporting currency.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.customerUsageType
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 10/16/2017, Version: 17.11, Message: Please use entityUseCode instead. The customer usage type for this transaction. Customer usage types often affect exemption or taxability rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.entityUseCode
member.summary: The entity use code for this transaction. Entity use codes often affect exemption or taxability rules.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.customerVendorCode
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 3/1/2018, Version: 18.3, Message: Please use `customerCode` This field has been renamed to `customerCode` to match documentation for other APIs related to exemption customers.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.customerCode
member.summary:
Unique code identifying the customer that requested this transaction.
When you specify a `customerCode`, AvaTax will look to see if a customer exists with this code in the exemption certificate system. If that customer exists, and if that customer has uploaded an exemption certificate that applies to this transaction, the relevant parts of this transaction that can use the exemption certificate will be treated as exempt.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.exemptNo
member.summary: The customer Tax Id Number (tax_number) associated with a certificate - Sales tax calculation requests first determine if there is an applicable ECMS entry available, and will utilize it for exemption processing. If no applicable ECMS entry is available, the AvaTax service will determine if an Exemption Number field is populated or an Entity/Use Code is included in the sales tax calculation request, and will perform exemption processing using either of those two options.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.reconciled
member.summary: If this transaction has been reconciled against the company's ledger, this value is set to true.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.locationCode
member.summary: DEPRECATED - Date: 3/1/2018, Version: 18.3, Message: In order to ensure consistency of field names, Please use reportingLocationCode instead. This field has been replaced by the reportingLocationCode field

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.reportingLocationCode
member.summary:
For customers who use [location-based tax reporting](https://developer.avalara.com/avatax/dev-guide/locations/location-based-reporting), this field controls how this transaction will be filed for multi-location tax filings.
If you specify a non-null value for this field, AvaTax will ensure that this transaction is reported on the tax return associated with the [LocationModel](https://developer.avalara.com/api-reference/avatax/rest/v2/models/LocationModel/) identified by this code.
This field does not affect any addresses for the transaction. It only controls the tax filing behavior of this transaction.
If you are looking for information about how to set up addresses for a transaction, please see [Using Address Types](https://developer.avalara.com/avatax/dev-guide/customizing-transaction/address-types/) in the AvaTax Developer Guide.

member.summary: A user-defined reference code for this transaction.

member.summary: The salesperson who provided this transaction. Not required.

member.summary: If a tax override was applied to this transaction, indicates what type of tax override was applied.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.taxOverrideReason
member.summary: If a tax override was applied to this transaction, indicates the reason for the tax override.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.totalAmount
member.summary: The total amount of this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.totalExempt
member.summary: The amount of this transaction that was exempt.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.totalDiscount
member.summary: The total amount of discounts applied to all lines within this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.totalTax
member.summary:
The total tax for all lines in this transaction.
If you used a `taxOverride` of type `taxAmount` for any lines in this transaction, this value may be different than the amount of tax calculated by AvaTax. The amount of tax calculated by AvaTax will be stored in the `totalTaxCalculated` field, whereas this field will contain the total tax that was charged on the transaction.
You can compare the `totalTax` and `totalTaxCalculated` fields to check for any discrepancies between an external tax calculation provider and the calculation performed by AvaTax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.totalTaxable
member.summary: The portion of the total amount of this transaction that was taxable.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.totalTaxCalculated
member.summary:
The amount of tax that AvaTax calculated for the transaction.
If you used a `taxOverride` of type `taxAmount` for any lines in this transaction, this value will represent the amount that AvaTax calculated for this transaction without applying the override. The field `totalTax` will be the total amount of tax after all overrides are applied.
You can compare the `totalTax` and `totalTaxCalculated` fields to check for any discrepancies between an external tax calculation provider and the calculation performed by AvaTax.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.exchangeRate
member.summary: The currency exchange rate from the invoice currency (`currencyCode`) to the reporting currency (`exchangeRateCurrencyCode`). This only needs to be set if the invoice currency and the reporting currency are different. It defaults to 1.0.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.isSellerImporterOfRecord
member.summary:
By default, the value is null, when the value is null, the value can be set at nexus level and used. If the value is not null, it will override the value at nexus level.
If true, this seller was considered the importer of record of a product shipped internationally.
If this transaction is not an international transaction, this field may be left blank.
The "importer of record" is liable to pay customs and import duties for products shipped internationally. If you specify that the seller is the importer of record, then estimates of customs and import duties will be added as tax details to the transaction. Otherwise, the buyer is considered the importer of record, and customs and import duties will not be added to the tax details for this transaction.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.deliveryTerms
member.summary:
Delivery Terms (or Incoterms) refer to agreed-upon conditions between a buyer and a seller for the delivery of goods. They are three-letter trade terms that describe the practical arrangements for the delivery of goods from sellers to buyers and set out the obligations, costs, and risks between the two parties. The DeliveryTerms field determines who acts as the Importer of Record and who arranges the Transport of the goods when this information is not separately sent to AvaTax in the request. When used in conjunction with isSellerImporterOfRecord, this parameter can also influence whether AvaTax includes Import Duty and Tax in the transaction totals. If the fields for transport or isSellerImporterOfRecord are passed in the request and conflict with the DeliveryTerms, the values provided in the first will take priority over the DeliveryTerms parameter. If neither transport nor isSellerImporterOfRecord is passed in the request and DeliveryTerms is passed, AvaTax will determine who acts as Importer of Record and who arranges the Transport of the goods based on the specified DeliveryTerms. If none of these fields is passed, AvaTax will keep the current behavior and default transport to "Seller" for goods and isSellerImporterOfRecord to "False" (i.e., the AvaTax user does not act as Importer of record)." Finally, this field is also used for reports.
The Delivery Terms that the user can pass are the following: 1. Ex Works (EXW) 2. Free Carrier (FCA) 3. Carrier and Insurance Paid to (CIP) 4. Carriage Paid To (CPT) 5. Delivered at Place (DAP) 6. Delivered at Place Unloaded (DPU) 7. Delivered Duty Paid (DDP) 8. Free Alongside Ship (FAS) 9. Free on Board (FOB) 10. Cost and Freight (CFR) 11. Cost, Insurance and Freight (CIF)
DAP and DDP are two delivery terms that indicate that Import Duty and Tax should be included in the transaction total.

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.apStatusCode
member.summary:
Users can set tolerance or threshold limits on transactions and inform users of appropriate actions to take if a transaction falls outside of these values. An Accounts Payable (AP) status code indicates the action that needs to be taken when the tolerance/threshold falls above or below the tolerance/threshold limits.
Available AP status codes are: 1. NoAccrualMatch 2. NoAccrualUndercharge 3. NoAccrualOvercharge 4. NoAccrualAmountThresholdNotMet 5. NoAccrualTrustedVendor 6. NoAccrualExemptedCostCenter 7. NoAccrualExemptedItem 8. NoAccrualExemptedVendor 9. NoAccrualRejectMatch 10. NoAccrualRejectUndercharge 11. NoAccrualShortPayAvalaraCalculated 12. NoAccrualRejectOvercharge 13. NoAccrualExemptedGLAccount 14. AccruedUndercharge 15. AccruedVendor 16. AccruedShortPayItemsMatch 17. AccruedShortPayItemsUndercharge 18. AccruedShortPayItemsOvercharge 19. NeedReviewUndercharge 20. NeedReviewVendor 21. NeedReviewMatch 22. NeedReviewOvercharge 23. PendingAccrualVendor 24. PendingAccrualUndercharge 25. PendingShortPayItemsUndercharge 26. PendingShortPayItemsMatch 27. PendingShortPayItemsOvercharge

member.name: P:Avalara.AvaTax.RestClient.TransactionModel.apStatus
member.summary: An Accounts Payable (AP) status indicates an action that needs to be taken when the tolerance amount falls above or below certain threshold limits.
